input_question: Psychosis aur Schizophrenia mein kya farq hai?
actual_answer: Psychosis ek syndrome ya symptoms ka majmua hai. Jo shakhs psychosis ke dauran hota hai woh haqeeqat se doori mehsoos karta hai. Psychosis ke aham symptoms mein hallucinations aur delusions shamil hain. Hallucinations aise ehsaasat hain jo haqeeqat nahi hotay, jaise awazen sunna jo asal mein nahi hotin. Awazain sunna aam hallucination hai, lekin hallucinations kisi bhi hiss ko mutasir kar sakti hain jaise sun'na, dekhna, boo mehsoos karna, zaiqa mehsoos karna, ya chhoona. Delusions mazboot yaqeen hain jo asal mein mumkin nahi. Mamool ki delusions mein shamil hain yeh yaqeen ke koi aapko peechay kar raha hai ya dekh rahaa hai, ya yeh yaqeen ke aap ke paas azeem taqatain hain. Psychosis ke doosre symptoms mein tawajju mein mushkilat, kaam mukammal karne mein mushkilat, ya faislay karne mein mushkilat shamil hain. Soch uljhi hui mehsoos hoti hai, kuch log guftagu ko samajhnay ya wazeh bolne mein pareshani mehsoos karte hain. Psychosis aap ke jism ke harkat ya jazbaat ke izhaar ko bhi mutasir kar sakta hai. Schizophrenia ek zehni bemari hai jo psychosis paida karti hai, lekin schizophrenia ke aur bhi symptoms hote hain. Aur psychosis sirf schizophrenia ki wajah nahi hota. Kuch cases mein dusri zehni bemariyan bhi psychosis paida karti hain, jaise ke depression, bipolar disorder, dementia, aur borderline personality disorder. Psychosis sakht dabao, neend ki kami, ya chot lagne ke dauran bhi ho sakta hai. Jo log kuch drugs ya davai se istamal kar rahe hain ya wapas le rahe hain unhe bhi psychosis ho sakta hai. Psychosis dimaghi chot, neurological masail, ya dusre health problems ki wajah se bhi ho sakta hai. To jab ke psychosis schizophrenia ka hissa ho sakta hai, lekin iska sabab bohat cheezein ho sakti hain. Psychosis aur schizophrenia dono ilaaj shuda hain. Jald se jald madad lena zaruri hai.
predicted_answer: Positive symptoms normal functions ki tahrib ko zahir karte hain, jin mein mazboot ghalat aqeeday, sunti hallusinations, aur dhila taluq shamil hain.
actual_reference: Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders Fifth Edition DSM-5
predicted_reference: Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders Fifth Edition DSM-5

input_question: Why do I need a plan?
actual_answer: There are a lot of things you cant control. You cant control what happens next, how governments respond, or how your neighbours react to the pandemic. What you can do is make a plan and decide how youll manage the things you do control, like your ability to stay safe, follow public health measures, stay connected with loved ones, and take care of your mental and physical health. Events like a pandemic change a lot over time, and that uncertainty can add to fear and stress. The truth is that we dont know what will happen nextbut that doesnt mean were all helpless. You can do a lot. You can Map out a daily schedule, including times you can connect with loved ones online or by phone Plan out daily tasks and goals Give yourself a schedule to look up current information Make sure you have 14 days of healthy food and household supplies at home Keep medications on hand and talk to your doctor for advice if youre at increased risk Educate yourself on public health measures and figure out how youll follow those instructions Figure out how youll manage increased childcare demands, working for home, or other changes to your usual routine Determine if youll need financial supports like the Canada Emergency Response Benefit or BC Temporary Rent Supplement if your job is affected by COVID-19. If you anticipate a need, you can find application instructions and gather any documents or other pieces of information ahead of time Figure out how youll manage times when you feel overwhelmed or hopeless, like calming activities, the number for a local support or crisis line, a video chat with a loved one, or a way to connect with your mental health care provider Look for local support organizations, neighbourhood groups, or mutual aid groups if you need extra help or support or would like to help others in your community Some planning will be straightforward, but you may also encounter situations or times when its harder to see a solution or good plan of action. This is a great time to practice problem-solving skills. Problem-solving is a method that helps you break down a complicated situation into manageable pieces, look for realistic and unbiased information, brainstorm possible solutions, and test the solutions that you think might work well. Problem-solving is a helpful skill no matter what else is going on in the world, and its an empowering, methodical approach when you might otherwise feel overwhelmed or lost. Check out the following resources for more information about problem-solving Wellness Module 4 Problem-Solving www.heretohelp.bc.cawellness-modulewellness-module-4-problem-solving Problem-solving worksheet from Anxiety Canada www.anxietycanada.comsitesdefaultfilesProblemSolving.pdf Effective Problem-Solving in The Antidepressant Skills Workbook psychhealthandsafety.orgasw
predicted_answer: It may be tempting to try to block out the world altogether to avoid bad news, but its important to keep yourself informed. We all have to step up during a pandemic because we all have a part to play in reducing the spread of the virus. Its important that you know what must be done and how you should do it. This is important for the health of your neighbours and your own mental health, and taking action can help counter difficult feelings like hopelessness and despair. One study from people in China found that people who had reliable up-to-date information about the coronavirus and COVID-19 illness and accurate instructions on how they should act such as instructions around hand-washing and wearing a mask felt more resilient and felt better able to handle the virus. People who received good, accurate information reported lower levels of stress, anxiety, and depression. Staying informed does not mean that you have to follow the news all day. Check in a few times a day, sticking to trusted sources and media outlets. While social media can be a great way to keep in touch with family and friends, social media can also amplify bad advice, vague or untrue stories, and other unhelpful information. Be sure to use good critical thinking skills.
actual_reference: World Health Organization Mental Health Manual

input_question: How can I see a psychologist?
actual_answer: To find a Registered Psychologist, visit the BC Psychological Association. You can make an appointment on your own. You dont need a doctors referral to see a psychologist. Registered Psychologists are not usually covered by MSP, so youll have to pay for the cost of the appointment yourself. Workplace extended health benefits or Employee Family Assistance Programs may cover some costs. Some psychologists offer a sliding scale based on your income. If youre facing financial hardship, you can ask when you make the appointment. If you want to learn more about lower-cost options to access counselling though not usually by Registered Psychologists, email us with your location and our information and referrals team can see what options there may be in your community. If you see a Registered Psychologist through a public mental health centre like a mental health team or outpatient psychiatry program, it will still be covered by MSP. Talk to your care provider if you have questions or want to learn more.
predicted_answer: A psychiatrist is a specialist doctor. In BC, you need to ask your doctor for a referral. If you dont see a regular family doctor, you can ask any doctor at a walk-in clinic for a referral. In some cases, you may be able to self-refer or make an appointment with a mental health program yourself, but that may not get you access to a psychiatrist. Contact your local mental health centre to see whats available in your area. If you need help finding your local mental health centre. also also you can talk to psycharist available online in our app

input_question: Anxiety aur anxiety disorder mein farq kya hai?
actual_answer: Anxiety hamari zindagi ke bohat se haadsaat aur halat par ek normal reaction hai. Anxiety aik internal warning system hai jo hume khatra ya dusray threats ka pata deta hai aur hamare jism ko fight, flight, ya freeze ke liye tayar karta hai. Scientists ise fight, flight, ya freeze response kehte hain. Waqt-be-waqt thodi anxiety madadgar ho sakti hai, jese test ki tayari ya kaam ke task ko mukammal karne ke liye motivate karna. Khushi ke lamhon jaise naya ghar lena ya badi khushi manani mein bhi anxiety aa sakti haiyeh sab insani fitrat ka hissa hain. Anxiety tab masla banta hai jab yeh azeem ho jaye, control se bahar ho, ya achanak ubhar aaye. Anxiety disorders aisi mental bimariyan hain jo aapki zindagi ko bari tor par mutasir karti hain. Log rozana ki zindagi se bachne ki koshish karte hain takay anxiety na ho. Unhein jism se juda zeeharat aur sehat ke masail mehsoos ho sakte hain. Bohat se log kehte hain ke unki anxiety haqeeqat par nahi mabni hai, lekin woh apne khayalat aur jazbat mein phansay hue hain. Anxiety disorders ilaaj ke laayak hain. Agar aapko anxiety ke baray mein fikr ho toh madad lain. Jab koi anxiety disorder se guzarta hai... Anxiety achanak bina wajah ubhar sakti hai. Taqreeban har situation mein anxiety ka response unki tawakko se zyada hota hai. Wo aise hazaro khof mehsoos karte hain jo shayad kabhi bhi nahin hotay. Anxiety kai der tak rehti hai, chahe masla hal ho gaya ho. Anxiety control hona mushkil lagti hai. Wo un cheezon se danay bachte hain jin se anxiety barhti hai. Yahan normal anxiety aur anxiety disorder ka ek misal hai. Bohat log hawai safar se thoda ghabraye hue hote hain jo bilkul normal hai. Lekin agar unhein kaam ke liye safar karna ho toh wo asani se plane par baith sakte hain. Anxiety disorder walay shaks ke liye yeh mumkin nahin ke wo hawai adda jayen, chahe usse uska job khone ka khatra ho.

input_question: Psychotherapy aur counselling mein kya farq hai?
actual_answer: Psychotherapy aur counselling aksar milte julte lafz hain aur aam tor par aik hi cheez ke liye istemal hote hain. Dono un professionals ko bayan karte hain jo talk-based approaches istemal karte hain takay kisi mental illness ya mental health problem se recovery mein madad mile. Bahut se mukhtalif professionals counselling ya psychotherapy de sakte hain, jinmein registered psychologists, registered clinical counsellors, psychiatrists, dusray therapists aur counsellors, family doctors, psychiatric nurses, aur faith leaders shamil hain. Kisi bhi masle par baat karna faidemand hota hai; bohat log paate hain ke sirf doston ya family se baat karna unko behteri mehsoos karne mein madad karta hai. Ek professional therapist ya counsellor zyada madad karta hai kyun ke uske paas training, tajurba, aur jazbati fasla hota hai kyun ke wo aapko jaanta nahi. Wo mukhtalif nazriyat use karta hai aap ko sunne, support karne, aur masail ya patterns ka approach karne ke liye. Misal ke taur par, cognitive-behavioural therapy ek psychotherapy hai jo yeh nazriya rakhti hai ke skills seekh kar apne soch aur amal ko badal ke aap apne jazbaat, sehat, aur mustaqbil ki soch aur rawaiye behtari laa sakte hain. Ek therapist aap ko ye skills sikhata hai aur practise karne mein madad deta hai. Bohat si psychotherapies hain jin ke liye acha evidence hai, jaise cognitive-behavioural therapy, interpersonal therapy, dialectical behaviour therapy, solutions-focused brief psychotherapy, narrative therapy, aur emotion-focused therapy. Aam tor par, therapy aur counselling ke kuch pehlu aik jaise hotay hain, chahe jo approach chunein: aapki umeedein sessions ke liye, aapki samajh aur therapist ki samajh ke darmiyan mail, aur therapist ke sath aapka trust aur rishta. Research se pata chala ke 50% behtari, clients mein jo sirf active listening aur non-directive supportive therapy se guzre, sirf client aur therapist ke rishtay ki wajah se thi. Psychotherapy kaam karti hai, magar jo professional aap chunte hain aur wo aapke values se kitna milta hai bohat zaruri hai. Professional dhoondte waqt poochna acha hota hai ke wo kisi professional organization ka member hai ya nahi, jaise agle hisay mein listed hain.

input_question: How can I manage grief?
actual_answer: While a lot of people think of grief in terms of losing a person or pet, grief can come up whenever you lose something important. This includes Losing security, like losing your job or wondering how long youll be able to pay rent Losing stability or routine, like finding yourself working from home or navigating childcare closures Losing your sense of safety, like fearing you or someone you love might end up with COVID-19 Losing your social relationships, like missing time with family and friends now that everyone must practice physical distancing or self-isolation Losing hope for the future, like feeling that life will never go back to normal Losing important goals, like finding your classes, sports competitions, or performances are cancelled for the foreseeable future Losing important milestone celebrations like graduation ceremonies and weddings Grief bring up complicated feelings. You might feel sad, angry, frustrated, fearful, or hopeless. You may have a hard time eating or sleeping, or feel very tense. You may feel overwhelmed and tired. You may wonder if life will ever feel normal again. Everyone grieves in their own way and their own time. Here are some strategies to try as you navigate your own journey. Acknowledge and express your feelings in a healthy way. Give your feelings a name and find healthy ways to express them, such as by talking with a friend, writing in a journal, or making art. Give yourself as much time as you need. Grief follows its own schedule. Give yourself permission to use this time to take care of your well-being. Let go of expectations, tasks, or other obligations that can wait. Seek support. Grief can feel very isolating, even though a lot of people are experiencing some sort of loss right now. Reach out to friends or family and share your feelings. Look for ways to help and support each other. Take care of yourself. Ignoring health and well-being can make difficult experiences feel worse. Eat as well as you can, try to get enough sleep, spend time outside if its safe for you to do so, and exercise regularly. Think about self-care activities or strategies that have helped you cope with challenging situations in the past and make time for those activities. Know that feelings of grief will pass. Grief may feel intense at times, but those feelings will become more manageable over time and will eventually pass. Connect with a mental health professional if youre having a hard time. If youre having a hard time getting through the day, coping in unhealthy ways, or having a hard time managing difficult thoughts or feelings, its a good idea to seek help from a professional like a psychologist or counsellormany now offer online or phone appointments. To find help For everyone BC Psychological Association Find a Registered Psychologist at www.psychologists.bc.cafind_psychologist BC Association of Clinical Counsellors Find a Registered Clinical Counsellor at bc-counsellors.org BC Mental Health Support Line call 310-6789 no area code for to learn about services in your area or just to talk with someone right now For young people Kids Help Phone Talk to a counsellor at 1-800-668-6868 or chat at kidshelpphone.ca available 247 Foundry Contact your local Foundry office at foundrybc.ca for Foundy Virtual and information about local resources for youth ages 12-24 Youth in BC Chat with a crisis line responder at youthinbc.com available every day from noon 100am That Discomfort Youre Feeling Is Grief in Harvard Business Review at hbr.org202003that-discomfort-youre-feeling-is-grief Grieving from the Canadian Mental Health Association at cmha.cadocumentsgrieving Coping with Grief and Loss from Mind Your Mind at mindyourmind.cawellnesscoping-grief-and-loss
predicted_answer: Distraction is a very valid tool to help you cope when everything feels overwhelming or when you feel lonely or isolated. If you dont have a lot of energy or focus right now, try low-effort distractions like watching TV, browsing Youtube, listening to a podcast or audiobook, playing a game on your phone, reading an easy book or magazine, or working on a simple art project. If you have more energy and focus, give yourself a to-do list every day you can clean and take care of projects around your home, work on hobbies, connect with family or friends, read a new book and catch up on your favourite TV shows. You can find interesting opportunities to take online courses from universities all over the world through MOOCs and other online learning platforms, you can learn a new language online or through apps, and you can learn new hobbies and activities. As more people have to practice social distancing or self-isolation, people are finding creative ways to bring the world into their homes you can tour museums and art galleries, Skype with a scientist, watch animals at zoos and nature preserves, and more. When normal schedules are disrupted, its easy to fall into unhelpful habits. Look for ways to keep yourself on track with healthier habits. You could set yourself goals every day or turn activities into a fun competition with friends or familywhoever takes the most language classes wins Many communities are using social media platforms like Facebook to organize support and help for neighbours. If you are healthy and its safe to do so, you can sign up to walk dogs, pick up groceries and household supplies, and help others who cant go out at the moment. This can be a great way to make new connections in your area, and helping others is good for your own mental health. Just be sure to follow good hygiene practices and physical distancingyour own health is important.
actual_reference: World Health Organization Mental Health Manual

input_question: Rapid cycling ka matlab kya hai?
actual_answer: Rapid cycling ka matlab hai ke jo shakhs bipolar disorder ya depression ka tashkhis hota hai wo ek saal mein chaar ya us se zyada dafa depression aur/ya mania ke episodes se guzarta hai. Rapid cycling kisi bhi waqt ho sakta hai jab koi bipolar disorder ka shikar holagbhag 10-20% log jo bipolar disorder ka tashkhis hota hai, kisi na kisi waqat rapid cycling ka samna karte hain. Aksar cases mein, rapid cycling khud-ba-khud door ho jata hai aur log phir lamba aur kam dafa hone wale episodes ke pattern par wapas aate hain.
predicted_answer: Yeh aam baat hai ke log apni dawa lena band kar dete hain jab woh mehsoos karte hain ke alamat qabu mein hain. Kuch log dawa ke side effects ki wajah se bhi use chhod dete hain, baghair samjhay ke aksar side effects ko asani se manage kiya ja sakta hai. Jabke dawa lena chhor dena uchit lagta hai, masla yeh hai ke ziadatar alamat wapas aa jate hain. Agar aap ya aapka bacha dawa le raha hai, to bohat zaruri hai ke aap apne doctor ke saath milkar treatment mein badlao ki faislay karein. Aap unse baat karein ke aap kaise kar rahe hain aur agar side effects hain jo aapko ilaaj jari rakhne se rok rahay hain. Woh aapke saath milkar in side effects ko kam karne ki strategies banayenge ya aise ilaaj ki mansuba bandi karenge jo aapke liye behtareen ho.
actual_reference: World Health Organization Mental Health Manual
predicted_reference: World Health Organization Mental Health Manual

input_question: Main aise teenager ki madad kaise kar sakta hoon jo hamesha akela rehta hai?
actual_answer: Baghair kisi faislay ke baat karne ke liye jagah dein, halki phulki activities saath mein karein, aur zarurat ho tou professional rehnumai ki naram salahiyat se taraf rujoo karweyn.
predicted_answer: Hum fitratan un logon ki madad karna chahte hain jo khud ko behaal mehsoos kar rahe hain. Jab apka koi aisa pyara jis ke sath physical health ka masla ho, madad kaise karni hai yeh wazeh lagta hai, lekin mental illness jaise depression mein aksar log unsure hotay hain ke behtareen madad kaise karein. Kuch tips hain: Aapko zaroori nahin ke samajhna pade ke woh shakhs kya guzarta hai, bas yeh tasleem karna zaruri hai ke yeh mushkil tajarba hai. Aap kuch keh sakte hain jaise, 'Mujhe maloom hai yeh tajarba ya symptoms aap ke liye dardnaak hain,' ya 'Mujhe afsos hai ke aap behaal hain.' Agar aap ne khud kabhi depression dekha hai, toh keh sakte hain, 'Mujhe pata hai aap kaisa mehsoos kar rahe hain.' Sab ko yaad rahe ke har shakhs depression ko alag tareeqay se mehsoos karta hai, aur hamdardi illness ke asraat ko samajhne ke liye hoti hai, symptoms ka muqabla karne ke liye nahi. Aapko expert honay ki zarurat nahin, lekin depression ke bare mein zyada seekhna madadgar ho sakta hai. Hamara Depression info sheet shuruaat ke liye acha hai, aur humare paas kaafi resources ki list hai. Kabhi kabhi problems ya fikr par baat karna baree madad hoti haibahut se log bas suna jana chahte hain. Hamesha yaad rakhein ke khul kar mushkil baatein karna aasaan nahin hota. Aap unhe baat karne ko nahin majboor kar sakte, lekin unhe aman aur khamosh jagah de sakte hain. Jab koi pyara aap se baat kare, to bina mpaka aur dhyan dhang se sunein. Hamesha hamdardi aur be-insaafi ke baghair sunein. Chahe aap masla samajh na paayein, aapka maksad parhna chahiye ke pyara kis dard se guzar raha hai. Family Toolkit ke Module Three mein communication aur sun'ne ke tips hain. Kuch log abhi puri tarah baat karne ko tayar nahin hote. Unki hudood ka ehtiram karen. Be-mang advice aksar madadgar nahin hoti. Aap shayad problem hal na kar saken, aur aapko poori kahani bhi maloom nahin hoti. Agar aap apne tajurbe share karna chahe, toh poochhein ke dosre ke liye yeh faidemand hoga ya nahi. Har shakhs ki zaruratein alag hoti hainhamisha yeh na samjhein ke aapko pata hai behtareen kya hai. Kuch log jazbati madad chahte hain, jaise kisi se baat karna. Kuch log counselor ya professional se achha talluq rakhte hain, lekin practical madad chahte hain, jaise ghar ka kaam ya ilaaj plan par amal karna. Aur kuch log sirf social events mein shamil hona chahte hain. Log jab depression mein hotay hain to aksar negative khayalat aur jazbat hotay hain jo dusron par sodium asar daalte hain. Aapke apne liye support lena acha haifamily aur friends ke liye alag support groups hain. BC Schizophrenia Society ke paas mental illness ke liye family support groups ka directory hai. Agar aapke pyare ko ilaaj pasand nahin ya dusray options chahye, toh aap unki madad kar sakte hain dusre resources or services dhundhne mein.
actual_reference: World Health Organization Mental Health Manual
predicted_reference: National Institute of Mental Health Clinical Guide

input_question: Dysthymia ya mustaqil mayoosi ka marz kya hai?
actual_answer: Dysthymia, ab jisay persistent depressive disorder ya PDD kaha jata hai, ek mood disorder hai. Yeh major depressive disorder se mutaliq hai. Dono mein farq tashkhis ke symptoms ki tadaad aur unka arsa hai. Jo shakhs PDD ka tashkhis hota hai woh kam az kam do saal tak niche diye gaye symptoms mein se do se chaar symptoms mehsoos karta hai bina kisi refa-e-halat ke dore ke, jabke major depressive disorder mein kam az kam do hafton tak paanch ya us se zyada symptoms hotay hain. PDD ke symptoms mein shamil hain: Ek ghalat fehmi hai ke PDD major depressive disorder jitna shadeed illness nahi hai, lekin tehqiqat dikhati hai ke is marez ki disability, maslan muntaazir kaam karne ki salahiyat aur shauq se lutf uthane ki salahiyat, PDD mein major depressive disorder ki tarah hi shadeed ho sakti hai. Kyunke PDD mein symptoms ki tadaad kam hoti hai lekin wo bohat arsay tak rehti hain, bhoot se log samajhne lagte hain ke PDD unke kirdar ka hissa hai na ke ek aisa marz jo moassar tor par ilaaj kiya ja sakta hai. Is liye agar aapko shak ho ke aapko PDD hai to zaroor doctor ya zehni sehat ke mahir se baat karein.
predicted_answer: Ek chronic qisam ka depressive mood disturbance jo major depression se kam shiddat rakhta hai magar kam az kam do saal tak musalsal rehta hai.
actual_reference: National Institute of Mental Health Clinical Guide
predicted_reference: Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders Fifth Edition DSM-5

input_question: Trichotillomania kya hai
actual_answer: Ek bal khinchnay ki beemari jo apne balon ko bar bar khinchnay ki wajah se hoti hai, jis se bal jharnay aur functional nuqsan hota hai.
predicted_answer: Zeheni bemariyan aise sehat ke halat hote hain jo shakhs ke khayalat, jazbat, rishtedari aur rozana zindagi ke funke munazzam karte hain. Ye pareshaani aur rozana zindagi ke mamul ke kam kaj mein kamzori se mutaalliq hoti hain. Zeheni bemariyan sakhti ke ek musir par hoti hain; kuch halki hoti hain aur sirf zindagi ke chand pehluon ko mushkil karti hain, jaise ke chand fobiyaan. Spectrum ke doosre kinaare par sangeen zeheni bemariyan hoti hain, jo bari ta'sir aur rozana zindagi mein badhawat paida karti hain. In mein bari depression, schizophrenia, aur bipolar disorder shamil hain, aur aksar shakhs ko aspatal mein ilaaj karna parta hai. Yeh zaruri hai ke jana jaye ke zeheni bemariyan tibbi halat hain jin ka shakhs ke kirdar, zaheenat, ya iradi quwwat se koi talluq nahin hota. Jaise ke diabetes pancreas ke fauad mein ek beemari hai, waise hi zeheni bemari dimagh ki biological halat ki wajah se hoti hai. Bil mushabahah jaise diabetes ka ilaaj dawa aur insulin se hota hai, zeheni bemari ka ilaaj dawaon aur samaji madad ke mubtadil tareeqe se hota hai. Ye ilaj bohat mofid hain, jahan 70-90 fisad afrad ko ilaj se apne alamat mein kami aur behtareen zindagi ka tajurba hota hai. Munasib ilaj ke sath, zeheni bemari shuda shakhs ke liye mustaqil aur kamyab hona bohat mumkin hai.
actual_reference: Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders Fifth Edition DSM-5
predicted_reference: World Health Organization Mental Health Manual

input_question: Main kaise jaanoon ke main zyada sharab pee raha hoon?
actual_answer: Yeh maloom karna ke aap zyada sharab pee rahe hain ya nahi mushkil ho sakta hai. Aap munfarid hain aur aapka sharab ke saath taluq bhi munfarid hai. Kisi ke paas aap ke jese zindagi ke tajurbe aur asraat nahi hote. Is liye aap aur aapke dost dono sharab pi sakte hain, lekin aap dono ke sharab ke istemal ka tareeqa aur wajah mukhtalif ho sakti hai. Jo log sharab pi'te hain wo faiday ke liye pi'te hain na ke mushkilaat ke liye. Magar kabhi kabhar hum zyadah aur ziada dafa istemal shuru kar dete hain jo maani nahi rakhta. Aam tor par, sharab peena masla tab banta hai jab yeh hamari ya doosron ki zindagi par manfi asar daalta hai. Bohat se log samajhte hain ke yeh sirf un logon ke liye baat hai jo har roz aur din bhar sharab peete hain. Magar ek dafa zyada sharab peena bhi masla paida kar sakta hai, jaise nashay mein gaari chalana jaisa ghalat faisla lena. Sab se aham yeh samajhna hai ke sharab peene se hone walay manfi nataij har context yani jagah, waqt, aur sar-garmi mein aur waqt ke saath farq kartay hain, jaise ke musalsal mushkilat se nijat pane ke liye sharab peena.
predicted_answer: Hamara info sheets mood disorders, bipolar disorder, aur depression ke baray mein maloomat deti hain, treatment options, aur resources ke sath. Humare paas 'What is Depression?' nam ka tool bhi hai jo adult depression ke signs ke sath bipolar disorder ke signs ko bhi screen karta hai. Agar aap basics se aage jana chahte hain, toh hamari Managing Depression series madad kar sakti hai ke aap depression ki tashkhees ke sath kaise deal karen, doctor ke sath achi tarah kaam karen, aur depression relapse se bachen. Aap wahan kai personal experiences aur perspectives bhi paenge jo aapko tanha mehsoos karne se bachayenge.
actual_reference: World Health Organization Mental Health Manual
predicted_reference: National Institute of Mental Health Clinical Guide
